channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Walter Tyndale: Celebrated Watercolorist and Documenter of Architecture and Culture
item.description: Walter Tyndale (1855–1943) was a prominent British watercolorist known for his detailed and atmospheric depictions of architecture, landscapes, and street scenes. Born in Germany and later residing in...

item.title: Nigeria Police warns public against fake ‘AFRIPOL 2026’ programme
item.description: The Nigeria Police Force don issue warning say make everybody no fall for one online programme wey dem dey call 'AFRIPOL 2026'. According to Deputy Commissioner of Police Anthony Placid, the Force nev...

item.title: Canadian officials defend Bill C-22 amid privacy and tech concerns
item.description: Canada's spy agency, CSIS, has raised concerns that failing to pass the government's proposed lawful access legislation, Bill C-22, could limit its ability to respond to threats and contribute to inte...

item.title: GlobalPort-PFP achieves unexpected victory at USPA Midwest Open Polo Tournament
item.description: In a surprising turn of events at the USPA Midwest Open, the GlobalPort-PFP polo team managed to secure a remarkable win, defying expectations from fans and sports analysts alike. The team’s performan...

item.title: Retired RCMP Officer Says He Was Misled Into CBC Satirical Prank Series
item.description: A retired RCMP officer, known online as Clinton Jaws, claims he and several former officers were misled into participating in a CBC-funded prank show called Northland Tales. The officers were reported...

item.title: ABS-CBN Presents the Word of the Day: Bloom
item.description: ABS-CBN, a leading media and entertainment company in the Philippines, continues its tradition of educational and engaging content by featuring the 'Word of the Day' for May 21, 2026, which is 'bloom....

item.title: Rosemary Zimu-Mnguni returns to acting with new role in Strings Attached after embracing motherhood and marriage
item.description: Rosemary Zimu-Mnguni makes her comeback to television after nearly two years away from the screen to focus on motherhood and marriage. The actress stars in the new Mzansi Magic drama series 'Strings A...

item.title: Philippines Reaffirms Sovereignty over Pag-asa and Lawak Islands Amid Chinese Claims
item.description: The Department of National Defense (DND) of the Philippines has firmly rejected recent assertions from China’s defense ministry claiming sovereignty over Pag-asa Island and Lawak Island in the West Ph...

item.title: The Life and Art of Georges Meunier, Celebrated Belle Époque Illustrator
item.description: Georges Meunier (1869–1942) was a renowned French poster artist and illustrator who vividly captured the lively spirit of Paris during the late 19th and early 20th centuries. A contemporary of Jules C...

item.title: Modi-Meloni ‘Melody’ moment in Italy sparks political clash between BJP and opposition in India
item.description: A light-hearted diplomatic gesture during Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Italy has unexpectedly triggered a sharp political exchange back in India. During his meeting with Italian Prime Minis...

item.title: Rivers State Governor Fubara Pulls Out of APC Governorship Primary, Pledges Support for Party Candidate
item.description: Rivers State Governor, Siminalayi Fubara don officially withdraw from the APC governorship primaries, as e don declare say e go support whoever fit eventually become the party candidate. The governor ...

item.title: Canberra woman receives $285,000 in damages after tripping on cracked floor tile
item.description: A 67-year-old woman has been awarded $285,000 in damages after tripping on a cracked tile in the foyer of Canberra's Hellenic Club. The fall, which occurred in November 2020 while she was visiting the...

item.title: SMILE Satellite Launched to Observe Sun-Earth Interactions and Auroras
item.description: The SMILE (Solar wind Magnetosphere Ionosphere Link Explorer) satellite, a joint mission between the European Space Agency (ESA) and China's Academy of Sciences, was successfully launched on May 19, 2...

item.title: Oyo School Principal’s Car Burnt After Terrorists Used Am to Abduct Pupils
item.description: For Esiele community inside Oriire Local Government Area, Oyo State, the past attack wey happen dey shake the community well well. According to the community chairman, Amusa Wakilu, terrorists wey car...

item.title: DA criticises Ekurhuleni for paying suspended officials while investigations continue
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has raised concerns over the City of Ekurhuleni spending more than R8 million on 17 suspended employees while disciplinary cases remain unresolved. The suspended staff inc...

item.title: Rising inflation and fuel prices put strain on South African households
item.description: South Africans are feeling the pinch as inflation jumped to 4% in April, up from 3.1% in March, bringing it to the midpoint of the South African Reserve Bank's 3%–6% target range. Economists warn that...

item.title: Jeff Bezos comments on Amazon's investment in Melania Trump documentary despite poor reviews
item.description: Jeff Bezos has addressed criticism regarding Amazon's backing of the documentary film 'Melania,' which has been widely panned by critics. Despite the movie achieving a mere 10% score on Rotten Tomatoe...

item.title: White House Prepares AI Executive Order as Google Unveils New AI Products at I/O Conference
item.description: The White House is set to release a highly anticipated executive order on artificial intelligence as soon as tomorrow, according to sources. The order is expected to outline key policy directions on A...

item.title: South Australia to feature in the Michelin Guide, boosting local food and tourism
item.description: South Australia is set to become the first Australian region included in the prestigious Michelin Guide, with the inaugural selection announced in October. Originally founded in France in 1900 to prom...

item.title: Travis Barker Documentary ‘Louder Than Fear’ Set for Tribeca Premiere and Global Release
item.description: Travis Barker, the renowned drummer of Blink-182, is the focus of a new documentary titled 'Louder Than Fear', which delves into his life, career, and personal struggles. The 90-minute film is set to ...
